key_risks: Hot Core PCE print on May 28 could spike yields and hit growth/semis disproportionately
Semiconductor profit-taking after extended 70% 6-month run could reverse the momentum trade
Taiwan geopolitical headline risk (cross-strait tensions) could trigger sudden EWT drawdown
Utility M&A regulatory pushback could pressure XLU after recent rally
Gold weakness continuing on JPM forecast cut and stronger dollar could drag the hedge sleeve

portfolio.rationale: Strong momentum (+3.59% 7d, +13.80% 30d), Nvidia data center revenue +85% YoY, Taiwan +6.09% confirms AI supply chain strength heading into PCE release.
Best 7d performer (+6.09%) with Q1 GDP +13.7% and exports +35.3% YoY; semis supply chain tailwind likely to persist through short trading week.
Strong 7d momentum (+3.73%); $1.5T FY27 defense budget proposal and Iran/Hormuz geopolitical risk provide near-term tailwind.
Defensive carry with +3.37% 7d momentum; NextEra/Dominion $67B M&A catalyst and weak consumer sentiment (44.8) favor defensives during holiday-shortened week with PCE risk.
Hedge against PCE upside surprise (Core PCE consensus 3.2% with CPI accelerating to 3.8%) and geopolitical Hormuz risk; recent -0.82% pullback offers entry.

portfolio_rationale: Barbell tilting toward strong-momentum AI/semis (SMH, EWT) and defense (ITA), balanced by defensive Utilities and Gold hedge against sticky inflation prints (PCE on May 28) in a holiday-shortened 4-day week. Avoids weak crypto, oil, and Communications which showed negative 7d momentum.
rationale_summary: Lean into the strongest near-term momentum (semis, Taiwan, defense) while hedging the May 28 Core PCE release and geopolitical tail risk with utilities and gold. Equity-heavy but with defensive ballast given Shiller CAPE at 41.6 and hot CPI print.
